title: Macrotyloma kasaiense
taxonomy: Macrotyloma kasaiense is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The plant is classified as a legume, which is a type of plant that produces seeds in a pod. Macrotyloma kasaiense is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The exact classification within the genus Macrotyloma is not specified, but it is likely to be placed within the tribe Phaseoleae.

intro: Macrotyloma kasaiense, also known as Kasai dolichos, is a plant species native to Africa, belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Macrotyloma kasaiense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Macrotyloma kasaiense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
